description: James McEwen joined the Ministry of Justice in 2016. He has held a number of roles over that time including Deputy Chief Executive, Legal Aid Agency; and Group Finance Director and Chief Operating Officer for the Ministry, providing leadership across the department’s professional functions as well as oversight of a number of the department’s Executive Agencies and Arm’s Length Bodies. From April 2024 James jointly led the Government Finance Function. Prior to joining the Ministry of Justice James built extensive experience working in the then Department of Energy and Climate Change (DECC). James began his Civil Service career in 2002 at the Department for Work and Pensions where he worked in a number of roles across policy, strategy and finance functions. From October 2025 he has been Director General Chief Executive Officer of HMPPS.

details.body:
James McEwen joined the Ministry of Justice in 2016.

He has held a number of roles over that time including Deputy Chief Executive, Legal Aid Agency; and Group Finance Director and Chief Operating Officer for the Ministry, providing leadership across the department’s professional functions as well as oversight of a number of the department’s Executive Agencies and Arm’s Length Bodies. From April 2024 James jointly led the Government Finance Function.

Prior to joining the Ministry of Justice James built extensive experience working in the then Department of Energy and Climate Change (DECC).

James began his Civil Service career in 2002 at the Department for Work and Pensions where he worked in a number of roles across policy, strategy and finance functions.

From October 2025 he has been Director General Chief Executive Officer of HMPPS.

James McEwen joined the Ministry of Justice in 2016.

He has held a number of roles over that time including Deputy Chief Executive, Legal Aid Agency; and Group Finance Director and Chief Operating Officer for the Ministry, providing leadership across the department’s professional functions as well as oversight of a number of the department’s Executive Agencies and Arm’s Length Bodies. From April 2024 James jointly led the Government Finance Function.

Prior to joining the Ministry of Justice James built extensive experience working in the then Department of Energy and Climate Change (DECC).

James began his Civil Service career in 2002 at the Department for Work and Pensions where he worked in a number of roles across policy, strategy and finance functions.

From October 2025 he has been Director General Chief Executive Officer of HMPPS.

The Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function (GFF) co-leads and develops the community of finance professionals across government, to help to maintain the highest standards of financial management and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively.

Key responsibilities include:

  • setting and delivering a GFF strategy for the development of the Government Finance Function
  • overseeing the professional development and performance of the GFF, including the Government Finance Academy curriculum and the annual continuing professional development plan
  • supporting departments’ talent management, succession planning and recruitment for the most senior financial roles in government

The Joint-Head of the GFF is accountable to Parliamentary select committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, for the continuing improvement of financial management standards, skills and expertise in government.

role.title: Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: James McEwen - Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

The Director General leads the COO Group, supporting the MOJ in delivering its priorities.

The COO Group includes:

  • Commercial & Contract Management
  • Finance
  • People Function
  • Project Delivery
  • Property
  • Security and Information Services

role_appointments.title: James McEwen - Chief Operating Officer and Director General of COO Group
